name: Peach and Blueberry Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Adjust the oven rack to about one-third of the way down and prepare a shallow 2-quart casserole dish by buttering it.
3. Blanch the peaches in boiling water for 15 seconds (if ripe) or longer if not quite ripe. The peaches are done when you can push your thumb across the surface, causing the skin to wrinkle.
4. Place the blanched peaches in a bowl of ice water and peel with fingers. Return partially peeled peaches to boiling water if necessary and slice into wedges.
5. Wash and drain the berries and set aside.
6. In a mixing bowl, sift together flour, 3/4 cup sugar, baking powder, and salt. Add the beaten egg and beat with a fork until all dry ingredients are incorporated.
7. Switch to a pastry blender or food processor and mix until well combined (no more mixing).
8. Sprinkle the topping evenly over the berries in the casserole dish.
9. Drizzle melted butter over the topping.
10. Mix the cinnamon with the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar and sprinkle over the top.
11. Bake uncovered for 15 minutes, then increase the temperature to 400°F and bake an additional 12-15 minutes until the topping is lightly browned.
12. The casserole may be prepared ahead of time, refrigerated, and baked while eating dinner after standing at room temperature for about half an hour.
